overview: The Line-Break Conversion step converts all line-breaks in an input document to a specified type (DOS/Windows, Unix/Linux, or Macintosh). It takes a raw document as input and sends back a raw document. The type of line-break in the input file is detected automatically, and the file may contain a mix of different types of line-breaks.

lineBreak.description: Select the new type of line-break for the output files. The value is an encoded string representing the desired line-break characters. Valid choices are: DOS/Windows (Carriage Return + Line-Feed, \r\n, 0x0D+0x0A) represented as '$0d$$0a$'; Unix/Linux (Line-Feed, \n, 0x0A) represented as '$0a$'; Macintosh (Carriage-Return, \r, 0x0D) represented as '$0d$'. The default is '$0a$' (Unix/Linux).
notes: The input line-break type is detected automatically and the file may contain a mix of different types

processingNotes: You must specify the input encoding of the document, even for formats where it is usually automatically detected (like XML, HTML, etc). The only exception is files with a Byte-Order-Mark.
The output is produced in the same encoding as the input document.
Byte-Order-Marks are preserved if they are present in the input.
The type of line-break in the input file is detected automatically. The file may contain a mix of different types of line-breaks.

examples.title: Convert to Unix line endings
examples.description: Convert a file with mixed or Windows line-breaks to Unix/Linux line-feed (\n) format. Set lineBreak to '$0a$' (the default value).
examples.output: All line-breaks in the output file will be \n (0x0A)

examples.title: Convert to Windows line endings
examples.description: Convert a file to DOS/Windows CR+LF line-break format. Set lineBreak to '$0d$$0a$'.
examples.output: All line-breaks in the output file will be \r\n (0x0D+0x0A)

examples.title: Convert to classic Mac line endings
examples.description: Convert a file to Macintosh carriage-return format. Set lineBreak to '$0d$'.
examples.output: All line-breaks in the output file will be \r (0x0D)
